items.headline: AI Optimism Drives Markets as Earnings Season Looms
items.summary: The stock market concluded a pivotal week marked by continued enthusiasm for artificial intelligence (AI) related shares and anticipation for the upcoming second-quarter earnings season. The S&P 500 is up 10.7% year-to-date and remains just 0.45% below its all-time closing high, signaling ongoing bullish sentiment. While easing oil prices have helped temper inflation concerns amidst persistent US-Iran tensions, rising 10-year Treasury yields, currently at 4.56%, present a potential threat to market stability, with a move toward 5% likely prompting a Federal Reserve rate hike. Narrow leadership was observed, with mega-cap technology stocks predominantly driving the S&P 500's gains, contrasting with the performance of the average stock.

items.headline: SK Hynix Debuts Strongly on Nasdaq
items.summary: South Korean semiconductor maker SK Hynix ($SKHYV) soared 13% (closing at $168.01, up from its $149 issue price) in its Nasdaq debut, re-energizing the AI trade and marking the largest foreign IPO to date.

items.headline: Meta Platforms Extends Gains
items.summary: Meta Platforms ($META) jumped 6% on Friday, extending its strong weekly performance and emerging as the top Magnificent Seven performer for the day.

items.headline: WD-40 Company Climbs on Strong Earnings
items.summary: WD-40 Company ($WDFC) climbed 11% following the release of its strong third-quarter results.

items.headline: Moderna Tumbles
items.summary: Moderna ($MRNA) experienced its worst day in over a year, tumbling almost 11%.

items.headline: Micron Technology Under Pressure
items.summary: Competitor Micron Technology ($MU) faced downward pressure, finishing 1.24% lower, potentially influenced by SK Hynix's strong debut in the memory-chip market.

items.headline: Materials and Communication Services Lead, Healthcare Lags
items.summary: On July 10, ten of the eleven primary S&P 500 sectors ended in the green. Materials led gainers with a 1.12% increase, followed by Communication Services, which rose 0.92%. Information Technology also showed positive movement, while Healthcare bucked the trend with a 0.81% decline.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Affirms Commitment to Price Stability
items.summary: The Federal Reserve delivered its semi-annual Monetary Policy Report to Congress, reiterating its commitment to achieving 'price stability.' Policymakers anticipate that current elevated inflation metrics, particularly service-sector price pressures, are expected to be transitory.

items.headline: June Payrolls Soften, Inflation Remains Sticky
items.summary: June payrolls came in at 57,000, roughly half of economists' expectations, with the unemployment rate ticking up to 4.2%. Despite softer labor data, the market reacted positively, pushing the odds of a September rate cut to around 80%. However, May's Consumer Price Index (CPI) registered 4.2% year-over-year, an increase from 3.8% in April, indicating persistent inflation.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season Kicks Off Next Week
items.summary: The second-quarter earnings season is set to begin next week, with major U.S. banks slated to report. Analysts are projecting S&P 500 earnings to surge by 24% from a year earlier, with technology companies expected to be a primary driver of this growth.
